name: brca_omics_cosine_warmup
notes: Baseline improvement 10/10: lr_schedule='cosine' with lr_warmup_epochs=10. The LR rises linearly from lr×0.01 (1e-6) to lr (1e-4) over the first 10 epochs, then decays via CosineAnnealingLR from lr to lr×0.01 over the remaining 190 epochs. The warmup addresses a known failure mode of Adam: at initialisation, the second-moment estimate v_t is near zero so the effective step size 1/sqrt(v_t+ε) is large — if the base LR is also large this can cause destructive updates before the model has accumulated any gradient history. Warmup builds up a meaningful v_t estimate before the full LR is applied. Combined with cosine annealing (shown to be beneficial in brca_omics_cosine_lr), this is the schedule standard in transformer pre-training and increasingly in small supervised models. All other settings identical to brca_omics_topk1000 (CI=0.6017).
